rag_rules: Latched expressions are only valid in component implementations, not in type contracts or assume/guarantee clauses
Binary 'and' expressions must have boolean types on both sides; fix type mismatches by correcting helper declarations or expressions
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must reference only visible identifiers or locally declared helpers; avoid package-qualified names not in scope
Assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes should relate visible inputs and outputs, not implementation-specific subcomponents
Use only AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations; avoid unresolved or invalid type references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks with valid component scope placement
Guarantee expressions must have consistent types; boolean results from comparisons, integer from arithmetic, real from real arithmetic
anti_patterns: Do not place latched expressions in component type contracts or assume/guarantee blocks; they are implementation-only
Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE expressions; remove or fix the root cause
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type vs implementation scope distinctions for subcomponent ports
Specific requirement traceability or compact semantics
